asymmetry: Student loan repayments now act as a 9% stealth tax on earnings above £25k, meaning a young parent on £35k loses £900/year extra compared to someone who never went to university — for a degree that was supposed to improve their life.
hook: Your degree is costing you more than you think.
core_fact: A graduate earning £35,000 pays £900/year in student loan repayments — someone without a degree pays £0. After 30 years the debt is wiped, but most graduates never clear it, meaning they paid a 9% tax surcharge their entire working life for nothing.
villain: The UK Government — which tripled tuition fees in 2012 and then lowered the repayment threshold in 2023, making graduates pay MORE per month than the original deal promised when they signed up.
share_trigger: Did you see this? They changed the rules AFTER people already took the loans — it's literally a retrospective pay cut.
search_query: UK student loan repayment threshold change 2023 graduates paying more BBC News
